title: Rochea coccinea
taxonomy: Rochea coccinea is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. Further, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Cymbidieae. Rochea coccinea is a genus of orchids and has been placed within the subtribe Coeliopsidinae. The species Rochea coccinea is native to Australia and is known for its distinctive red flowers. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.

intro: Rochea coccinea, commonly known as the red rochea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. Rochea coccinea is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments, including wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rochea coccinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
